timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Trump assassination attempts coverage (per user instruction). Subtopic canonical keyword names Butler explicitly but the cluster spec covers both attempts (Butler Jul 13 + Florida Sep 15); both are included. Contributing articles directly from user-provided list: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/7/13/donald-trump-live-news-ex-us-president-walked-off-stage-after-shots-heard (Jul 13 liveblog) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/14/donald-trump-rally-shooting-minute-by-minute-timeline-of-what-happened (Jul 14 timeline — AJ's most structured chronology) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2024/7/14/donald-trump-rally-shooting-live-coverage (Jul 14 liveblog) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/14/trump-shot-at-pennsylvania-rally-in-assassination-attempt-what-to-know ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/14/trump-doing-well-after-being-shot-in-right-ear-at-pennsylvania-rally ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/14/timeline-assassination-attempts-against-us-presidents-candidates ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/14/what-does-the-trump-rally-attack-mean-for-the-us-election ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/15/how-is-the-attempted-assassination-of-donald-trump-being-investigated ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/15/biden-trump-stress-unity-after-assassination-attempt ; https://www.aljazeera.com/gallery/2024/7/16/us-politics-blood-on-the-campaign-trail ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/18/hes-got-a-gun-the-60-minutes-leading-up-to-trump-assassination-attempt ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/21/us-secret-service-admits-failures-in-review-of-shooting-at-trump-rally ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/5/trump-to-rally-in-butler-pennsylvania-site-of-attempted-assassination ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/6/fact-checking-donald-trumps-claims-at-butler-rally . Additional AJ articles drawn from broader coverage to anchor key accountability milestones not in user-provided list: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/22/us-secret-service-chief-faces-grilling-in-congress-after-trump-shooting (Cheatle Jul 22 hearing) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/7/23/secret-service-director-cheatle-resigns-amid-uproar-over-trump-shooting (Cheatle Jul 23 resignation) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/15/donald-trump-safe-after-attempt-on-his-life-at-florida-golf-course (Florida Sep 15) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/9/16/who-is-ryan-routh-the-man-arrested-in-second-trump-shooting-incident (Sep 16 Routh charging) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2024/10/21/house-task-force-says-secret-service-failed-at-trump-butler-rally . Opinion piece read for context only (no entry drawn): https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2024/7/14/the-trump-assassination-attempt-a-race-reset-and-more-polarisation . Date approximations per rule 4: Four same-day entries on Jul 14 preserved as separate distinct events (the minute-by-minute reconstruction, Trump's medical status update, the historical context piece, and the election-impact analysis are materially different AJ articles) — multiple entries per date allowed when each captures a different news development per rule. Two Jul 15 entries similarly preserved. Routh's charging (Sep 16) — Florida state firearm charges were filed Sep 16; federal attempted-assassination charges were upgraded Sep 24; AJ entry uses Sep 16 as anchor for initial charging since AJ's coverage that day used 'attempted assassination' framing reflecting prosecutors' early characterization. Cheatle resignation (Jul 23) — well-established date, her resignation letter was issued the morning of Jul 23 following her Jul 22 testimony. Source disagreements: Shooter's motive — at the time of AJ's coverage in July-September 2024, the FBI had not publicly attributed a definitive motive to the Butler shooter; AJ entries reflect that uncertainty and do not characterize motive. Number of casualties — at Butler one rally attendee was killed and two were wounded; entries do not detail private-individual victims per rule 6. Public figures named: Trump, Biden, Cheatle, Routh — all public officials/figures of record (Routh is a public defendant in a federal case, named in indictment). Shooter at Butler (deceased perpetrator) was officially identified as Thomas Matthew Crooks but is not named in any entry to keep focus on accountability milestones and avoid foregrounding the deceased perpetrator. Private individual victims (rally attendees killed/wounded at Butler) deliberately not named per rule 6, even though their identities were publicly reported.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's Trump assassination attempts coverage (per user instruction). Contributing articles from user-provided list (in cluster window): https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/13/politics/trump-injured-pennsylvania-rally ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/election-biden-trump-07-13-24 (Jul 13 liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/14/politics/timeline-trump-rally-shooting-dg (Jul 14 CNN timeline article — primary chronology anchor) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/14/politics/what-happened-trump-assassination-attempt ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/14/politics/trump-rally-shooting-distance (Jul 14 130-yard distance reporting) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/14/politics/what-was-said-on-stage-after-trump-was-shot ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-rally-shooting-07-14-24 (Jul 14 liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-assassination-shot-07-16-24 (Jul 16 ongoing liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-rally-shooting-07-18-24 (Jul 18 RNC speech coverage) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/26/politics/fbi-trump-interview-assassination-attempt ; https://www.cnn.com/politics/live-news/trump-assassination-attempt-election-news-09-16-24 (Sep 16 post-Florida liveblog) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/25/politics/trump-return-butler-pennsylvania-assassination-attempt ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/21/politics/trump-assassination-attempt-house-task-force-report . Additional CNN articles drawn from broader coverage to anchor key accountability milestones: https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/22/politics/cheatle-house-oversight-testimony-trump (Cheatle Jul 22 hearing) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/07/23/politics/cheatle-secret-service-director-resigns (Cheatle Jul 23 resignation) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/09/15/politics/trump-florida-golf-course-shots (Florida Sep 15) ; https://www.cnn.com/2024/10/05/politics/trump-butler-rally-return-coverage (Butler return). Articles dropped as out of cluster window per rule 5: https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/13/politics/trump-butler-assassination-maga-change ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/12/politics/secret-service-trump-assassination-attempt ; https://www.cnn.com/2025/07/09/politics/secret-service-suspensions-agents-butler-pennsylvania-trump — all are July 2025 anniversary or follow-up pieces well after the Jan 31, 2025 cluster end. Date approximations per rule 4: Four same-day entries on Jul 14 preserved as distinct events (timeline article, 130-yard-distance reporting, on-stage account, what-happened detailed account are all materially different CNN articles published the same day). Two Jul 13 entries similarly preserved (the initial news report and the ongoing liveblog coverage). Jul 16 entry pegged to CNN's Jul 16 liveblog covering the post-shooting investigation status — at that point the shooter's motive remained publicly unclear. Sep 25 entry pegged to CNN's article date about Trump's planned Butler return (the actual return rally was Oct 5, captured as separate entry). Source disagreements: Distance from shooter to Trump — CNN's Jul 14 reporting cited 'roughly 130 yards' (about 120 meters); other outlets and the eventual House task force report cited 'approximately 400 feet' (133 yards) — entry preserves CNN's Jul 14 reporting per rule. Shooter motive remained publicly uncharacterized by FBI throughout cluster window; entries reflect that. Public figures named: Trump, Biden, Cheatle — all public officials. Shooter (Crooks, deceased perpetrator) not named in any entry. Private individual victims at Butler (one killed, two wounded) not named per rule 6. Routh is mentioned in AJ's timeline (c6_d_t1 entry 13) but not in CNN's; CNN's coverage of his charging is captured in the Sep 16 liveblog entry without naming him.
